agent_notes: Quality-constrained longlist: Ealing has strong pubs, wine bars, markets and two approved western-park walk anchors, but limited qualifying bookshop-cafe and current-exhibition depth.

Creative Walks only use Gunnersbury Park and Osterley Park & House from the PAINTS v6 safety list; Walpole Park, Lammas Park, Pitshanger Park and canal routes beyond Little Venice were not sourced as walks.
Google ratings/review counts and group walk-in tolerance need host re-check before publishing because live Google Maps pages are not reliably extractable here.

venues.opening_hours: Wed-Sun 10:00-17:00; first Thu 10:00-20:00; Mon/Tue closed except bank holidays. July 2026 partial closures and main-gallery changeover flagged by venue.
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed entry/book-now available; walk-in admission may be possible but group 8-12 and timed-entry availability are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a hosted gallery day if tickets are checked in advance; cafe/shop usable without ticket, but group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house and contemporary gallery; circulation works better as a paced 2-hour visit than one static table.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10:30-12:30 or first Thursday early evening for resident/pay-what-you-can atmosphere.
venues.why_fits_paints: A rare Ealing anchor where architecture, contemporary art and a real local story meet. Marthe Armitage's pattern-making exhibition gives the host a strong craft narrative rather than a generic gallery stroll.
venues.anecdote_story: Sir John Soane designed Pitzhanger as his country home; the current Marthe Armitage show links west-London craft, Chiswick studio practice and hand-printed wallpaper design.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Marthe Armitage: Pattern Maker to 19 July 2026; Clare Woods: Garden Without Seasons opens 29 July 2026. Cafe and shop on site.
venues.safety_notes: Central Ealing, daytime/early evening access to Ealing Broadway transport; avoid routing attendees through unapproved parkland after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min; closest qualifying alternative is Gunnersbury Park Museum at about 25-35 min by transit/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Main gallery closures/changeovers in July 2026 make exact date verification essential.

venues.opening_hours: Museum Tue-Sun 10:00-16:30; Mon closed except booked school visits and bank holidays. Park daily 07:00-dusk.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free-entry museum; group 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a lightly hosted local-history and design session if the special exhibition is open; group handling should be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Permanent local-history rooms plus special exhibition gallery; cafe in park.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Rothschild-house setting and west-London social history give it more texture than a generic local museum. It is strongest when paired with a Gunnersbury creative walk.
venues.anecdote_story: Gunnersbury House was formerly a Rothschild country estate and reopened after major restoration with displays about Ealing and Hounslow.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent west-London collection; special-exhibition programme exists but exact current July 2026 exhibition is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.safety_notes: Approved Premium Launch park; daytime only for park sections, with clear transport exits.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min; closest qualifying alternative is Pitzhanger at about 25-35 min by transit/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition claim is the weak point; do not publish as an exhibition-led PAINTS event without checking the live programme.

venues.venue_name: Gunnersbury Park House, Temple and Lake Walk
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; meeting/weather backup cafe walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 in daylight; keep route compact and finish before dusk.
venues.capacity_notes: Route stops: museum facade/Rothschild house, Italian garden/temple, round pond/lake, orangery or museum threshold.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the safest Ealing creative-walk anchor because it is explicitly PAINTS-approved and visually varied. The host can move between grand-house history, designed landscape and local collection stories.
venues.anecdote_story: The estate moved from private country house to public park in 1926; 2026 marks the centenary of public ownership.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, historic house exterior, lake views and optional museum displays.
venues.safety_notes: Gunnersbury Park is on the Premium Launch Parks safety list. Daytime route only; do not extend into unvetted canals or isolated evening paths.
venues.backup_venue: Pure in the Park Cafe / Gunnersbury Park Museum cafe
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of museum-side start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather backup is cafe/museum rather than same-category walk; group seating at cafe is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: National Trust estate and house hours vary by season; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact July 2026 house, garden and cafe times.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open parkland/garden route with paid house/garden components; group entry and cafe walk-in are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a slower daytime route; not suitable as an Ealing-core evening walk.
venues.capacity_notes: Route stops: Robert Adam house exterior, garden approach, lake/parkland view, stables/cafe area.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday late morning or early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: A beautiful west-London designed-landscape route that clears the PAINTS safety list, but sits just outside the strict Ealing core. Best used for a Southall/outer-west cohort rather than Ealing Broadway.
venues.anecdote_story: Osterley is associated with Robert Adam's neoclassical remodelling and National Trust stewardship of a former country estate.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, historic house/garden and optional National Trust interiors.
venues.safety_notes: Osterley Park & House is on the Premium Launch Parks safety list. Keep to daylight and formal visitor routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of recommended start point
venues.risks_watchouts: Adjacent-borough caveat and National Trust seasonal/ticketing complexity keep this Tier B.

venues.walk_in_policy: Independent wine bar/shop; walk-ins appear part of normal bar model, but group 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely viable early Wed/Thu or Sat opening if host calls ahead; avoid Friday peak.
venues.capacity_notes: Small wine-bar format; confirm table/standing space before matching a full group.
venues.why_fits_paints: A neighbourhood wine shop-bar with seasonal bottles and a relaxed non-chain texture. It gives show-and-tell guests an easy object to discuss: the bottle label, producer and why it was chosen.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue positions itself around wines not found on supermarket shelves and a friendly shop-to-glass model.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal wines by the glass; shop bottles available to drink in for corkage.
venues.safety_notes: Northfields Avenue has tube/bus access and active frontage; late finish route should be kept to Northfields station or main bus corridors.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small footprint may struggle with 12 without host arrangement.

venues.opening_hours: Mon-Tue 07:00-16:00; Wed-Sat 07:00-22:00; Sun 07:00-15:00; food/drink last orders listed by venue.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available and normal cafe/wine-bar use appears open; group walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best early evening Wed/Thu, or daytime as Books & Coffee-adjacent backup; confirm seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Cafe by day, wine shop/bar by night; pavement and indoor seating noted by press/reviews.
venues.why_fits_paints: Pulp is unusually flexible for PAINTS: coffee, bakery, wine shop and small plates without feeling templated. The recent press signal points to genuine neighbourhood pull rather than generic west-London gloss.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue has built a small local identity around scrollsants, coffee and wine in South Ealing.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee, bakery, small plates, wine and wine-shop bottles.
venues.safety_notes: South Ealing high-street setting near tube; late walk should stay on main roads.
venues.risks_watchouts: More cafe-wine hybrid than pure drinks; group seating should be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Wed-Thu 17:00-22:00; Fri 16:00-22:30; Sat 15:00-22:30; Sun 14:00-19:00; Mon closed; Tue closed/private hire possible.
venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub model suggests walk-ins, but group 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; small-room peak risk.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best at opening on Wed/Thu/Sun; call ahead for a group of 12.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent one-room micropub with craft beer, real ale and community focus.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Dodo has the strongest operator story in the district and a real community identity. It is more characterful than convenient, exactly the PAINTS tradeoff.
venues.anecdote_story: Founder Lucy Do and the 'Hanwell Massive' community around the pub have been profiled by beer culture press.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Rotating real ale, craft beer, cider, wine and low/no options.
venues.safety_notes: Boston Road/Hanwell route should be kept to main roads and rail/bus links after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small capacity and popularity make it operationally fragile without a host call.

venues.opening_hours: Instagram indicates open until 21:00 Wed-Sat and shop until 17:00 Sun; exact hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram states 14 covers can be booked and a couple of walk-in seats exist; group 8-12 likely requires booking, so host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better as an intimate 8-person event than 12; operationally sensitive.
venues.capacity_notes: Small wine bar linked to Park + Bridge wine shop.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 18:00-20:00 with advance host contact.
venues.why_fits_paints: A tiny Acton wine room with a real specialist-wine story and sister shop nearby. Good for label-led show-and-tell rather than a loud pub night.
venues.anecdote_story: Press describes Vindinista as created by Paola Tich of nearby Park + Bridge, with a focus on small producers and unusual bottles.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Small-producer wines, deli plates and bottles from the sister wine shop.
venues.safety_notes: Churchfield Road is active and close to Acton Central; keep finish before late-night lull.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capacity and walk-in limitation keep this just under the highest operational confidence.

venues.walk_in_policy: Pub accepts ordinary walk-in custom; 8-12 table/group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if seated away from peak dining; book/heads-up advised.
venues.capacity_notes: Large historic pub with heritage interior; more robust than micropubs.
venues.why_fits_paints: A handsome Grade II-listed pub with enough visual and architectural detail to sustain a show-and-tell conversation. Stronger for heritage and atmosphere than wine specificity.
venues.anecdote_story: Built in 1909 by Nowell Parr and listed for its historic pub interior; recently refurbished sympathetically.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask beer, craft keg, Belgian bottles, wine and Thai food.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Northfields/West Ealing; use main-road route to Northfields or West Ealing stations.
venues.risks_watchouts: Fuller's managed-house status makes it less independent than the wine bars/micropubs.

venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub walk-in culture, but group 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; small seating and sharing tables are likely.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for 8 at opening; 12 is risky without arrangement.
venues.why_fits_paints: Small, local and unpolished in the right way, with serious beer culture and no chain sheen. A good fit for a low-cost, conversational PAINTS drinks event.
venues.anecdote_story: Part of west-London micropub culture; beer guides note six handpumps and London-brewed beer signals.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask beers, keg taps, cider, wine and soft drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Northfield Avenue is active and close to tube/bus routes; avoid late dispersal into side streets.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capacity and crowding; not ideal for a 12-person group at peak.

venues.opening_hours: Website lists Mon-Wed 12:00-23:20 and similar pub hours; full weekly detail is partially truncated in source, so UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub accepts normal custom; group 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable off-peak due to larger pub format.
venues.capacity_notes: Grade II-listed 1904 pub with historic interior and horseshoe bar.
venues.why_fits_paints: A sturdy Hanwell backup/primary with genuine historic-pub bones. It is less curated than the wine bars, but easier operationally for 8-12.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA identifies it as a Grade II-listed 1904 pub with a historic interior and horseshoe bar.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask beer including London Pride and changing beers; pub drinks and events.
venues.safety_notes: Hanwell residential setting; use main routes to Hanwell station or buses.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less distinctive drinks curation; check kitchen/current operator details.

venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a moving market day with a clear meet point and split-free browsing rhythm.
venues.capacity_notes: Producer/farmer focus; not all stalls attend weekly.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the cleanest Ealing market fit: producer-led, daytime, community-shaped and easy to discuss through food origin stories. It is not a car-park filler market.
venues.anecdote_story: London Farmers' Markets notes rotating stall attendance and proximity to local West Ealing food shops.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, prepared food and weekly market stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime West Ealing shopping area; keep meeting on active streets rather than Deans Gardens, which is not on the Creative Walk safety list.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15-20 min by bus/transit; outside ideal 15 min market backup radius, so host should treat as operational fallback not same-walk backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and variable stall attendance; meeting point must not rely on unapproved park space.

venues.opening_hours: Wed-Sat 10:00-16:30; weather closures possible; winter break late Dec-late Jan.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for daytime market wander with food stalls and maker/community elements.
venues.capacity_notes: Social-enterprise market with food, fresh produce, fashion/homeware and trader training/community role.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market's social-enterprise backbone and trader-development story give it more meaning than its simple high-street setup suggests. It works best when maker/food traders are programmed that week.
venues.anecdote_story: Action West London operates the market and explicitly supports new and developing traders.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, produce, fashion, homeware, social-enterprise and occasional maker stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Busy town-centre market by Morrisons; daytime only for PAINTS.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8-10 min walk when Maker's Market is operating
venues.risks_watchouts: Not every day has enough craft/maker identity; check current trader mix and weather alerts.

venues.opening_hours: Event-market dates/times vary; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm next operating date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access event market when operating; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if date is confirmed; weak as a recurring always-on option.
venues.capacity_notes: Advertised as 40+ independent traders, local artisans and sustainable businesses.
venues.why_fits_paints: This has the strongest craft/maker fit among Acton markets. It should be used only when the actual date and trader list are live.
venues.anecdote_story: Your Acton BID frames it as a curated Churchfield Road market with handmade goods, art, fashion, condiments, wines and food stalls.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent makers, jewellery, art, fashion, home decor and artisan food.
venues.safety_notes: Churchfield Road is an active neighbourhood high street; daytime market event only.
venues.risks_watchouts: Date dependency: if no upcoming market date is confirmed, do not publish.

venues.opening_hours: Ealing Council/Visit London indicate Southall Market opens Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat or Wed/Fri/Sat with Saturday busiest; exact July 2026 hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access street market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a culturally rich daytime market walk if host actively manages pace and meeting point.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy high-street market with food, clothing, jewellery/electronics and Southall Broadway context.
venues.why_fits_paints: Not polished, but culturally specific and story-rich. It is a Tier B PAINTS option because curation is weaker and operational clarity is messier.
venues.anecdote_story: Londonist describes it as one of London's last 'proper' markets rather than a curated food-hall style setup.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food stalls, Asian/European clothing, jewellery, electronics and Broadway street life.
venues.safety_notes: Very busy daytime high-street setting; avoid evening and define a precise meet/end point near station or main road.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10-15 min by bus/taxi; not a same-walk backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Operational hours vary across sources; not as maker/producer-led as PAINTS ideal.

venues.opening_hours: Facebook/secondary sources indicate Sunday market around 08:00/08:30-15:00/15:30; exact hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting-point cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible for a bargain/food-market daytime format; transport logistics make it less elegant.
venues.capacity_notes: Large outdoor/wholesale-market setting; less intimate than PAINTS ideal.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has local colour and food-market energy, but feels more logistical than curated. Keep as an outer-west Tier B rather than a launch hero.
venues.anecdote_story: The market sits within the wider Western International wholesale-market landscape near Southall/Hayes.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Sunday general market, street food and bargain stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; arrival/departure route should be pre-briefed because it is less pedestrian-friendly than Ealing/Acton centres.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10-15 min by bus/taxi; not a same-walk backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Adjacent-to-Ealing location, variable source quality and weather exposure.

venues.opening_hours: Reported as 07:00-16:00 seven days; current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Cafe model suggests walk-ins, but 8-12 seating for a 2-hour stay is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for 8, daytime, with soft pacing; 12 may overwhelm seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent coffee-and-books cafe; second-hand books noted by local listing.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Ealing's cleanest Books & Coffee fit because books are part of the venue identity, not a decorative shelf. It has the low-key, quiet, browse-and-talk energy the category needs.
venues.anecdote_story: Local writing and listings describe Paperback as a coffee haven with books and a calm South Ealing atmosphere.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee, sandwiches/pastries, local apple juice/wine references in social posts, and browsing books/second-hand books.
venues.safety_notes: South Ealing daytime high-street route; avoid stretching into unapproved Lammas Park as an event route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a full-scale bookshop; confirm current book offer and group seating before publishing.

venues.venue_name: Pitshanger Bookshop plus Pitshanger Lane coffee partner
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-ins accepted for retail browsing; paired coffee partner and 8-12 two-hour seating are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works only as browse-then-coffee hybrid; not as one single seated bookshop-cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop without verified on-site cafe; category fit depends on nearby independent coffee partner.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 10:30-12:30 if coffee partner can seat group.
venues.why_fits_paints: A real independent local bookshop with community texture, but it only partly clears Books & Coffee because coffee is not verified on site or literally next door. Use as a softer Tier B fallback.
venues.anecdote_story: Bookshop-focused blogs describe Pitshanger as a relaxed local shop with interesting books beyond bestseller tables.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Children's books, fiction, non-fiction and local bookshop browsing; coffee partner UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Pitshanger Lane is village-like and active; avoid treating nearby parks as Creative Walks because they are not on the approved list.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying Books & Coffee backup within 10 min; closest qualifying alternative is Paperback at about 20-25 min by transit/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Fails strict one-premises bookshop-cafe ideal; host must verify nearby coffee partner before using.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Tempting because it sits by Pitzhanger, but it is not on the PAINTS v6 Premium or Safety-First park list; not sourced as a Creative Walk.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list, despite proximity to Northfields/South Ealing venues.
Not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; cannot be used as a sourced walk or meeting-point park.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Grand Union Canal / Hanwell locks route
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 only permits Grand Union/Regent's Canal constraints around Little Venice-Camden/Angel; Grand Union beyond Little Venice is explicitly not permitted.
Chain bookshop; fails PAINTS independent/no-chain filter and no clearly PAINTS-coded on-site independent coffee offer.
Useful cultural venue/cinema-bar, but not a museum/gallery day with paintings, sculpture, textiles, ceramics or craft exhibition meeting the current-exhibition rule.
Strong independent cultural backup for Acton drinks, but not a qualifying Books & Coffee venue because the book offer is not substantive.
Good local arts organisation, but current July 2026 exhibition/press support could not be verified to the v6 museum-gallery threshold at run time.
Attractive Ealing Green pub, but weaker PAINTS category fit than the sourced micropubs/wine bars; independent/chain status and current group walk-in tolerance require verification.
Characterful Victorian pub, but private-hire/large pub positioning and less distinctive drinks story made it a weaker PAINTS fit than Forester, Dodo, Owl, Ealing Wine Co, Pulp and Vindinista.
